behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both file and environment, startup fails with an actionable error that names the exact missing config path (for example `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: a unit test of only the validator can miss failures in file->env->merged-config handoff and error rendering. REAL-INFRA: only local env/filesystem are needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocking config sources can hide path-flattening/key-path bugs. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use a temp config directory with a YAML file that omits `database.host`, clear `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST`, run the real loader entrypoint, assert it exits/fails and the message includes the exact path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: If `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves config path under that directory instead of `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: pure unit tests can miss env/path resolution wiring issues. REAL-INFRA: requires process env + local filesystem only. MOCK-HIDE: mocked path resolver may always pass while real resolution is wrong. No external network/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp dir, place config at `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/tool/config.yaml`, ensure home config path is absent/different, invoke loader, and assert loaded values come from the XDG location.

behaviors.description: When `TOOL_` environment variables conflict with YAML values, environment values take precedence.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: precedence bugs often appear in merge order across loader modules, not in one function. REAL-INFRA: local env + file inputs only. MOCK-HIDE: mocked config objects can bypass real normalization/casting order.
behaviors.test_description: Create YAML with `database.host: file-host`, set `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=env-host`, run real loader, assert resulting config uses `env-host`.

behaviors.description: YAML syntax errors are surfaced with the detected line number in the actionable error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: unit tests with fake parser exceptions can miss mismatch between parser error metadata and CLI message formatting. REAL-INFRA: real YAML parser + local file read; no network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocking parser would trivially provide a line and hide real parser behavior differences.
behaviors.test_description: Write malformed YAML (known bad line) to temp config file, run loader, assert failure message includes the parser-reported line number.

behaviors.description: On first run when config directory is missing, loader creates the directory and writes defaults before continuing.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: directory creation/write ordering and permissions are cross-module filesystem side effects. REAL-INFRA: requires real local filesystem behavior but not external processes/APIs. MOCK-HIDE: fs mocks can miss path-creation and write sequencing bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Point config root (via temp HOME or `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`) to a non-existent path, run startup loader, assert directory now exists, defaults file is created with expected baseline keys, and loader proceeds successfully.
